iter_label: PH4 slice-2 (frontend half) tag-chip click filter v0.6.0
iter_summary: Rewired mind/static/mind.js so tag-chip clicks fetch /api/graph?tag=<csv> server-side and redraw the D3 graph (no more CSS dimming for tag filter). Introduced baseGraph (unfiltered, drives sidebar pills + tag-cloud) vs currentGraph (rendered, may be filtered). applyTagFilter is async, short-circuits to baseGraph when activeTags is empty. SSE graph-change reload re-fetches both base + filtered slice so active filter survives a _shared-memory/ change. Stats badge shows [filter: <tags>]; title-status flips to 'filtered'. Bumped __version__ 0.5.0 -> 0.6.0 + pyproject.toml + description.

tests.framework: pytest + node syntax check + Flask test_client
tests.http_e2e: /api/graph 727n/265e; /api/graph?tag=$5-per-day-cap 2n/0e; CSV ?tag=a,b honored; empty ?tag= falls through to identity

source_health.frontend_split: baseGraph (unfiltered for sidebar) + currentGraph (rendered, possibly filtered)

next_iter_for_mind_agent: PH5 shortest-path UI polish: highlight path-edges with auto-zoom to both endpoints (existing /api/path endpoint is sufficient).
PH6 Ruflo agentdb semantic-similarity edges (graceful fallback to tag overlap when MCP not loaded, per CLAUDE.md).
PH4 polish: 'clear all chips' button next to the tag-cloud header (currently operator must toggle each chip off).
Consider exposing /api/health graph cache stats in the sidebar as a tiny perf badge (hits/misses).
